summary: Outstanding audience recording captures a fiery 1983 upstate‑NY show with a Crazy Fingers tease, soaring Bird Song and an electrifying Drums→Space sequence.
review: The tapes, recorded by Rick and Tom (often cited as “Coyotte”), deliver crystal‑clear audience sound that even preserves a rare 15‑second Space‑Odyssey tuning before Terrapin. Musically the concert is hailed as one of the best fall‑tour outings – Jerry’s worn‑out yet snarling guitar work, Bob’s graceful vocals, and highlight moments like Crazy Fingers into China Cat, Bird Song and an inspired Drum/Space jam make it a must‑listen.

ai_rating.rationale: All seven reviews are strongly positive, praising both the performance (standout songs, energetic playing) and the audio quality (crisp audience capture). The weighted average of show (≈4.5) and recording (≈4.3) yields a 4.4 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear, well‑balanced audience mix; occasional venue echo noted but overall crisp. Includes an unusual ~15 sec Space‑Odyssey tuning before Terrapin that most other versions omit.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opener with a snarling Shake‑and‑Bake into Wang Dang Doodle, followed by a second set that builds to a soaring Bird Song and an intense Drum/Space climax.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as sounding worn out yet snarling and graceful; his Crazy Fingers tease is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for graceful vocal work alongside Jerry’s guitar, especially on the opener and Bird Song.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drum segment leading into Space receives specific praise.
